relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph could no longer hide himself, and the room was cleared for truth.
His weeping filled Egypt’s hearing.
I am Joseph shattered the brothers into silence.
He brought them near and named the wound: you sold me into Egypt.
Then he opened a larger frame: the Divine sent me ahead to preserve life.
The famine was not finished.
Joseph had been carried ahead to preserve a remnant and a great escape.
Human betrayal was held inside divine sending without erasing responsibility.
He sent word to Jacob: come down, do not delay.
Goshen would place the family near Joseph.
Joseph would sustain them through the famine.
Their own eyes, and Benjamin’s eyes, could see that Joseph was speaking.
They were to tell Jacob Joseph’s glory in Egypt.
Joseph and Benjamin wept on each other’s necks.
Kisses and tears opened speech between brothers again.
Pharaoh’s house rejoiced at Joseph’s brothers.
Pharaoh sent them to bring the family.
Egypt’s good land was offered to them.
Wagons were sent for children, women, and Jacob.
They were told not to cling to what they would leave behind.
Joseph supplied wagons and provisions.
Garments returned in abundance, especially to Benjamin.
Joseph sent rich provision to his father.
He warned the brothers not to unravel on the way.
They returned to Jacob in Canaan.
The news that Joseph lived was too much for Jacob’s heart to believe.
The words and wagons revived Jacob’s spirit.
Israel chose to go and see Joseph before death.
